title: Data Sharing as Research Problem: Privacy-Enhancing Technology and Human-Computer Interaction
section: 1. Privacy-Enhancing Technology
text: 1. Privacy-Enhancing Technology

title: Data Sharing as Research Problem: Privacy-Enhancing Technology and Human-Computer Interaction
section: Essay, not a Paper
text:
Essay, not a Paper

“As a democratic, civil-rights respecting nation, it is irresponsible for our government agencies to continue to collect and disseminate datasets without careful consideration of the privacy implications of data sharing.”
“While there may appear to be an irreconcilable tension between facilitating data-driven insight and protecting the privacy of individual’s data, an emerging scientific consensus shows that Privacy-Enhancing Technologies (PETs) offer a path forward.”

title: Data Sharing as Research Problem: Privacy-Enhancing Technology and Human-Computer Interaction
section: 2. Human-Computer Interaction
text: 2. Human-Computer Interaction

title: Data Sharing as Research Problem: Privacy-Enhancing Technology and Human-Computer Interaction
section: This paper has three key aims:
text:
This paper has three key aims:


to gain insights into the data sharing practices of fertility trackers by revealing the nature of their data transmissions


to further develop a nuanced understanding of user preferences and attitudes towards data sharing in highly intimate contexts

and from these insights, we seek (3) to provide actionable recommendations to integrate meaningful transparency and control mechanisms in the design of fertility trackers, so they better align with users’ needs and desires.

How to DP-fy Your Data: A Practical Guide to Generating Synthetic Data With Differential Privacy

title: How to DP-fy Your Data: A Practical Guide to Generating Synthetic Data With Differential Privacy
section: How to DP-fy Your Data
text:
How to DP-fy Your Data
In this survey we explore the full suite of techniques surrounding DP synthetic data, the types of privacy protections different generation approaches can offer, and the state-of-the-art for various modalities including image, tabular, text and federated (decentralized) data. We outline all the components needed in a system that generates DP synthetic data, from sensitive data handling and preparation, to tracking the use of synthetic data and empirical privacy testing.
See Also: ICML 2025 Workshop

title: How to DP-fy Your Data: A Practical Guide to Generating Synthetic Data With Differential Privacy
text: In this survey we explore the full suite of techniques surrounding DP synthetic data, the types of privacy protections different generation approaches can offer, and the state-of-the-art for various modalities including image, tabular, text and federated (decentralized) data. We outline all the components needed in a system that generates DP synthetic data, from sensitive data handling and preparation, to tracking the use of synthetic data and empirical privacy testing.

title: How to DP-fy Your Data: A Practical Guide to Generating Synthetic Data With Differential Privacy
section: DP Synthetic data
text:
DP Synthetic data

division of labor between data engineer and data analyst
data engineer creates DP synthetic dataset
data analyst works with data that they can understand, with the benefit that it’s private
alternative: data engineer and analyst collaborate to models that are useful and private, but neither understand what the other is talking about

title: How to DP-fy Your Data: A Practical Guide to Generating Synthetic Data With Differential Privacy
section: DP-fy tabular data
text:
DP-fy tabular data
ICML Tutorial: Tabular data starting at 17:41

histogram
Select-Measure-Estimate paradigm, workload-based methods, tension between rigor & scalability (Sec 3.3)
alt: LLM-based methods (Sec 3.7)
evaluation: statistical fidelity of marginals, pairwise attribute distribution similarity, pairwise correlation similarity, standard statistical tests (Kolmogrov-Smirnov, Chi-square), distributional metrics (Sec 3.8)

title: How to DP-fy Your Data: A Practical Guide to Generating Synthetic Data With Differential Privacy
section: DP-fy tabular data … just tell me what to do
text:
DP-fy tabular data … just tell me what to do
ICML Tutorial: Tabular data starting at 32:21

if you want to match marginals => workload-based
better off using marginal based methods (MWEM+PGEM, AIM) if you don’t expect extremely high degree interaction between columns
consider LLM-based methods where histogram-based methods won’t be able to discover connections that LLMs already know based on column names & values - these are small data regimes

title: How to DP-fy Your Data: A Practical Guide to Generating Synthetic Data With Differential Privacy
section: DP-fy text data with DP finetuning
text:
DP-fy text data with DP finetuning

finetune an LLM with DP-SGD to generate data (Sec 5.2)
“workhorse method that is best for most cases”
LoRA tutorial on Building a Text Classifier
Google Deepmind synthetic data for text Colab Tutorial
skip Private evolution, DP inference methods for synthetic data
MAUVE score measures distribution similarity (Sec 5.6)
